Core Function

Write the ROM Data,
Restore 20V Charging

Corrupted USB-C ROM data causes the MacBook motherboard to receive only 5V. The S-BOS3 rewrites the exact OEM ROM data for your model — instantly restoring full 20V boost charging.

5V
Damaged State
→
S-BOS3
ROM Write
→
20V

Smart Identify · Auto Program · One-click write

Chip Compatibility

Two Chip Types,
Fully Covered

S-BOS3 contains pre-loaded OEM ROM data for both CD3215 and CD3217 chips across all MacBook generations with USB-C ports.

2016 – 2018
CD3215
Found in MacBook Pro and MacBook Air early USB-C generations. S-BOS3 carries all model-specific ROM variants.
2019 – 2023
CD3217
Used in M1, M2, M3 and T2 MacBook platforms. Updated ROM data preloaded for all variants.

Type-C MacBook 2016–2023 · iPad · iPhone

1
Insert the CD Chip
Place the CD3215 or CD3217 chip into the precision socket. The clamp mechanism secures alignment automatically.
2
Smart Identify
S-BOS3 reads the chip and auto-identifies the model. Match with your MacBook or iPad model from the menu.
3
Auto Program
Press Auto Program. The tool writes the correct OEM ROM data in seconds — no PC required.
4
Reinstall & Test
Reinstall the chip. Connect charger — the motherboard now negotiates 20V successfully.

Power Design

Type-C 5V
Power Supply

The S-BOS3 itself is powered via a standard USB-C 5V cable — the same type as the cables you're repairing. No separate power adapter needed. Built-in ON/OFF switch for convenience.
